title: PROMs: Patients’ feedback on specialist doctors at T0, T1 and T2. Data refer to the whole population, as well as to relative groups arriving at HCV diagnosis through general, harm reduction services/prison or specialist physicians. For each aspect analysed, PROMs were based on Likert scales (from 1 to 6 or from 1 to 5 or from 1 to 3) or binary responses (Yes/No). In the Likert scales, HCV patients expressed the level of agreement, where the minimum value represents the most disadvantageous feedback related to the relative outcome perceived. For binary and not binary responses, the Cochran’s Q and the Friedman tests were conducted, respectively. Post-hoc Dunn and Wilcoxon tests were performed when the Cochran’s Q test and the Friedman test were statistically significant, respectively. The significance level was set at p<=0.017.
